title: calliandra haematomma var. locoensis
intro: Calliandra haematomma var. locoensis is a variant of the Calliandra haematomma plant, a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, also known as the "powder puff flower" due to its clusters of small, powdery, red flowers resembling a woman's powder puff. The medicinal properties of Calliandra haematomma var. locoensis include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and treat digestive issues such as diarrhea and dysentery. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a tea, infused in hot water, or used as a tincture to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
